title: Roepera billardierei
taxonomy: Roepera billardierei is classified within the family Sapindaceae, a group of plants commonly known as the soapberry family. This classification is based on its morphological characteristics and genetic relationships with other plants. The genus Roepera is a part of the Sapindaceae family and is native to Australia and New Zealand. Roepera billardierei is a shrub species within this genus. It is a member of the broader order Sapindales, which includes various families of flowering plants.
uses: Roepera billardierei, also known as the coast orris, is a plant native to Australia. intro: Roepera billardierei, also known as the 'billardiere's roepera', is a shrub native to Australia, characterized by its small, white flowers and narrow, linear leaves. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help alleviate conditions such as arthritis and promote overall well-being. The bioactive constituents of Roepera billardierei include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties.
